title: 以下、Kernel/FUGUEに活用できる？ Server: SimpleHTTP/0.6 Python/3.11.9
summary_text: Top critique for using `openai/skills` in Kernel/FUGUE: 1. **Implicit invocation non-determinism** — **Critical** Risk: Different lanes may auto-trigger different skills from similar wording, causing divergent plans/actions and hard-to-reproduce outcomes. Mitigation: Disable implicit invocation for high-impact skills (`allow_implicit_invocation: false`), require explicit skill IDs in lane specs, and record invocation provenance per lane. 2. **Prompt drift in `SKILL.md` / references** — **High** Risk: Small instruction edits change behavior silently across runs, breaking reproducibility and regression expectations. Mitigation: Pin skill versions (commit SHA), hash `SKILL.md` + referenced assets, and fail runs when hashes mismatch approved baseline. 3. **Unsafe script side effects** — **Critical** Risk: Skill `scripts/` can execute toolchains with filesystem/network side effects that exceed lane intent. Mitigation: Capability allowlists per lane, sandboxed execution profiles, mandatory dry-run mode for mutating skills, and signed/approved skill sources only. 4. **Provider behavior divergence** (Codex vs Claude/GLM lanes) — **High** Risk: Same skill text yields materially different outputs, creating false consensus or hidden conflict. Mitigation: Provider-specific acceptance tests, structured output schemas, adjudication rules that compare normalized artifacts (not prose), and veto on schema mismatch. 5. **Provider lock-in via skill semantics** — **High** Risk: Skills become coupled to one provider’s invocation semantics/tools, reducing portability and resilience during outages. Mitigation: Keep orchestration contract provider-neutral, isolate provider adapters, and maintain parity fixtures run across `Codex + Claude + GLM`. 6. **Governance and audit gaps** — **Critical** Risk: No clear ownership/approval trail for skill changes enables policy drift, privilege creep, and incident forensics failure. Mitigation: CODEOWNERS for skills, mandatory review gates, change classification (low/high risk), immutable run ledger (lane, skill SHA, tool calls, outputs). 7. **Single-lane fallback disguised as progress** — **Critical workflow violation** Risk: If multi-model lanes drop and work continues, quality/safety claims are invalid under Kernel policy. Mitigation: Hard gate: if baseline quorum (`Codex + Claude + GLM`, or approved fallback with evidence) is missing, status must be `BLOCKED`. Minimum missing tests to add now: - Deterministic replay test: same inputs + same skill SHA => equivalent structured outputs. - Prompt drift test: reject unreviewed hash changes in `SKILL.md`/references. - Implicit-invocation boundary test: confirm restricted skills never auto-run. - Provider parity test: compare normalized outputs across Codex/Claude/GLM. - Policy gate test: enforce `BLOCKED` when quorum or lane minimum is unmet. - Side-effect containment test: skill scripts cannot access disallowed paths/tools.
